title: Stdin pipe input: accept plan JSON and open browser
description:
Accept databricks bundle plan JSON via stdin pipe and serve the webview.

USAGE PATTERN:
  databricks bundle plan -o json | dagshund
  # or from a file:
  cat plan.json | dagshund

WHAT EXISTS:
- src/index.ts — Bun server entry point (currently just serves static HTML)
- parsePlanFromString in parser/ — validates JSON string into Plan

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Detect stdin pipe in src/index.ts — if process.stdin is not a TTY, read all stdin to string
2. Parse the stdin JSON using parsePlanFromString
3. Inject the parsed plan into the served HTML as a script tag: window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ = <json>
4. Auto-open the browser after server starts (use Bun.spawn or child_process to open localhost URL)
5. Frontend (src/frontend.tsx or App.tsx) reads window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ on mount and feeds it to the graph

FALLBACK: If no stdin, serve the app anyway with empty state (for dev mode / future file upload)

DATA CONTEXT:
- Plan JSON can be large (hundreds of resources with nested tasks and changes records)
- The window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ global holds the raw parsed Plan object
- The rendering layer (dagshund-qu9) reads this and passes it through the graph model

CONSTRAINTS:
- Handle parse errors gracefully — print to stderr and exit with code 1
- Use Result type from types/result.ts
- No global mutable state — read stdin once, inject into HTML response
- Server should pick an available port if default is taken (nice to have, not blocking)
close_reason: Stdin pipe input implemented: server reads stdin, parses with Zod, serves at /api/plan. Frontend fetches dynamically with loading/empty/error/ready states. Auto-opens browser on pipe.

title: Hide unused connection handles on leaf nodes
description: TaskNode renders left (target) and right (source) Handles unconditionally. Nodes with no incoming edges (like set_env) show a dot on the left side with nothing connecting to it. End-leaf nodes show a dot on the right. Only render the target Handle when the node has at least one incoming edge, and the source Handle when it has at least one outgoing edge.

title: Project scaffold: Vite + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind + React Flow + Zod
close_reason: Scaffolded project: Bun + React 19 + TypeScript strict + React Flow + Tailwind v4 CLI + Zod. Directory structure per CLAUDE.md. Clean build verified.

title: Define scope and priorities for next features
description: Discussion task: define what the user wants in the visualizer before building more. Topics may include graph layout, React Flow rendering, UI/UX, interaction model, file upload vs stdin, etc.
close_reason: Scope defined: graph model (dagshund-ebk) → rendering (dagshund-qu9) + stdin (dagshund-1z4) in parallel. PyDABS testing tracked as dagshund-5f6 backlog.

title: Test dagshund with PyDABS-generated bundle plans
description: Validate that dagshund correctly parses and visualizes plan JSON generated from PyDABS dynamic bundle definitions. PyDABS generates Databricks asset bundle YAML dynamically, which then feeds into databricks bundle plan. Ensure the plan output from these dynamically-generated bundles renders correctly — edge cases may include unusual resource structures or dependency patterns that static YAML wouldn't produce.

title: Implement Zod parser + types for plan JSON
Add test infrastructure and parser tests
Fix bun --hot HMR reload loop with elkjs
description: bun run dev triggers continuous hot reloads when elkjs is in the dependency graph. The HMR re-bundles layout-graph.ts (~2600ms each) in an infinite loop. Production build works fine. Likely a bun HMR bug with large bundled dependencies. Workaround: use bun run build + static server for now.
close_reason: Verified resolved — no HMR reload loop observed with elkjs in dependency graph. User confirmed no flickering in browser.

title: Improve node alignment in fan-out/fan-in DAG patterns
description: Nodes in fan-out/fan-in patterns (e.g. data_quality_pipeline) are not perfectly centered on their layer grid. Single nodes like aggregate_results and generate_report can appear off-center relative to their incoming edges. Current ELK config uses BRANDES_KOEPF BALANCED with model-order options which is decent but not perfect. Investigate further ELK tuning or post-layout adjustment to improve vertical centering of nodes relative to their connections.

title: Nodes collapse to (0,0) when hovering after zoom-out
description:
When zoomed out fully and hovering over a node, all nodes collapse to position (0,0) in the top-left corner of the viewport. This was also seen earlier when the requestAnimationFrame ResizeObserver patch was active, but it's now happening without it.

Likely cause: the hover styling logic in DagView applies opacity changes via styledNodes/styledEdges useMemo. Something in that code path or in React Flow's internal re-measure cycle may be resetting node positions when the viewport scale is small enough.

title: Replace vendored dagre.js with proper dependency
description:
The vendored src/vendor/dagre.js (2200 lines of CJS-to-ESM shim) is a hack to work around @dagrejs/dagre v2's ESM dist using dynamic require() that Bun's browser bundler can't trace. Investigate and test alternatives in order of least invasive:

1. **Direct import test** — try importing @dagrejs/dagre directly (no vendor) to see if Bun 1.3.8 handles it now
2. **esbuild prebundle as build step** — `bunx esbuild node_modules/@dagrejs/dagre/dist/dagre.cjs.js --bundle --format=esm --platform=browser --outfile=src/vendor/dagre.js` as a reproducible build:vendor script instead of a committed 2200-line file
3. **esm.sh CDN import** — `import dagre from 'https://esm.sh/@dagrejs/dagre'` to bypass bundler entirely (downside: requires internet)
4. **Switch to elkjs** — proper ESM, recommended by React Flow, more capable. Async API (returns Promise). ~30 line migration.

Test each option against `bun build` and `bun run dev`. Pick the least invasive one that works. If none of 1-3 work cleanly, go with elkjs.
close_reason: Superseded: switching to elkjs instead of fixing dagre vendor shim

title: Add a complex multi-job fixture for testing
notes:
Current test fixture is a single job with a handful of tasks. A richer fixture would help stress-test layout, hover dimming, and the detail panel.

## Structure

**Job 1: ETL_PIPELINE (existing job, mostly unchanged)**
- Keep current tasks as-is
- Add a new task that triggers Job 2 via run_job_task referencing ${resources.jobs.DATA_QUALITY_PIPELINE} (DAB cross-job linking syntax)
- This trigger task should be 'added' diff state (new resource)

**Job 2: DATA_QUALITY_PIPELINE (new complex job)**
- 8-10 tasks with realistic Databricks names (validate_schema, check_nulls, compute_statistics, etc.)
- Deep dependency chain (4+ levels) to test ELK layout spacing
- Fan-in pattern: multiple validation tasks feed into a single aggregation task
- Fan-out pattern: one setup task spawns several parallel checks
- Mix of diff states: some added, some modified, one removed, rest unchanged
- Tasks with large change payloads (long field lists) to exercise detail panel scrolling
- Realistic notebook_task / python_wheel_task / run_job_task types

## Goal
Test cross-job edges, complex layout, and all visual states in a single fixture.

title: Detail panel: show full resource state, not just changes
description:
Currently the detail panel only shows diff changes — clicking an unchanged node like 'load' shows nothing useful. The panel should display:

1. **Current state** — all fields/config of the resource as they exist (baseline)
2. **Updates** — fields that changed, showing before → after
3. **Additions** — new fields that were added

This gives full visibility into what a resource looks like, not just what changed. For unchanged nodes the panel should still show the full resource configuration. For modified nodes it should highlight which fields changed within the full context.

title: Migrate from dagre to elkjs for graph layout
description: Replace @dagrejs/dagre (unmaintained since 2015, broken ESM packaging) with elkjs (actively developed, proper ESM, recommended by React Flow). This removes the 2200-line vendor shim entirely. Migration: elkjs has async API so usePlanGraph hook changes from useMemo to useState+useEffect. Files: src/graph/layout-graph.ts, src/hooks/use-plan-graph.ts, src/vendor/dagre.js (delete), src/types/dagre-cjs.d.ts (delete), package.json.
close_reason: elkjs migration complete, -2169 lines, production build verified in browser

title: Visualize non-job resources grouped by Databricks container type
description: Databricks bundle plans contain resources beyond jobs — volumes, schemas, genie spaces, registered models, etc. Each resource type belongs to a logical container in the Databricks hierarchy (e.g. volume → catalog.schema, schema → catalog, genie space → workspace). Dagshund should recognize these resource types and group/render them by their container category, similar to how job tasks are grouped inside job containers today.

title: Build graph model: Plan to React Flow nodes and edges
description:
Transform a parsed Plan (from parser/) into React Flow-compatible nodes and edges. All pure functions, no React code.

WHAT EXISTS:
- Plan type with record of PlanEntry (keyed by resource path string like 'resources.jobs.etl_pipeline')
- Each PlanEntry has: id, depends_on, action, changes, new_state, remote_state
- DiffState type: added | removed | modified | unchanged
- mapActionToDiffState function in parser/map-diff-state.ts
- Two real fixtures: sample-plan.json (all creates) and mixed-plan.json (updates/skips/removals)

KEY INSIGHT — THE DATA HAS THREE LEVELS:
1. Resource level: top-level plan entries keyed like 'resources.jobs.etl_pipeline'. Each has an action (create/update/delete/skip).
2. Task level: inside new_state.value.tasks, each task has task_key, depends_on (array of {task_key}), notebook_task, etc. These form the actual DAG the user wants to see.
3. Change level: the 'changes' record has keys like 'tasks[task_key=extract].notebook_task.notebook_path' with per-field actions (update/skip/delete). This tells us WHICH tasks and fields changed.

THE GRAPH MODEL MUST:
- Extract tasks from new_state.value.tasks as individual graph nodes
- Build edges from each task's depends_on array (task_key references)
- Determine each task's DiffState by checking the changes record:
  - If changes has 'tasks[task_key=X]' with action=update and only a 'new' field (no 'old') → added
  - If changes has 'tasks[task_key=X]' with action=update and only an 'old' field (no 'new') → removed
  - If changes has 'tasks[task_key=X].some.field' with action=update → modified
  - If all changes for task X are action=skip → unchanged
  - If the resource-level action is 'create' → all tasks are added
- Also produce a job-level metadata node showing the resource itself (name, triggers, etc.) — this is context, not part of the task DAG edges
- Handle multiple jobs in the plan (each is a separate sub-graph)

WHAT TO BUILD in src/graph/:
1. Graph types in src/types/graph-types.ts:
   - GraphNode: id, label, nodeKind ('job' | 'task'), diffState, resourceKey, taskKey?, raw data ref
   - GraphEdge: id, source, target, label?
   - PlanGraph: { readonly nodes: readonly GraphNode[], readonly edges: readonly GraphEdge[] }
2. extractTaskNodes(resourceKey: string, entry: PlanEntry) → readonly GraphNode[]
   - Dig into new_state.value.tasks, create a node per task
   - Compute diffState per task from the changes record
3. extractTaskEdges(resourceKey: string, entry: PlanEntry) → readonly GraphEdge[]
   - For each task with depends_on, create source→target edges using task_key
4. extractJobNode(resourceKey: string, entry: PlanEntry) → GraphNode
   - A single node representing the job itself with its resource-level diffState
5. buildPlanGraph(plan: Plan) → PlanGraph
   - Iterate all plan entries, call the above for each, combine into one graph
6. toReactFlowElements(graph: PlanGraph) → { nodes: Node[], edges: Edge[] }
   - Convert to React Flow format with dagre-computed positions
   - Must handle hundreds of nodes

FIXTURES TO TEST AGAINST:
- tests/fixtures/sample-plan.json — single job, action=create, 4 tasks (extract→transform→load→validate)
- tests/fixtures/mixed-plan.json — single job, action=update, changes include: transform notebook_path updated, aggregate task added (new only), validate task removed (old only), extract/load unchanged (all skips)

CONSTRAINTS:
- All readonly types, all pure functions, no classes
- Functions under 20 lines, verb-first camelCase naming
- Use Result type for anything that can fail
- No any, no type assertions without comment
- Dagre is a new dependency — @dagrejs/dagre
- new_state.value is typed as unknown in the schema — will need careful narrowing (Zod parse or type guard) to extract tasks safely
close_reason: Graph model complete: types, task extraction, diff state resolution, plan graph builder, dagre layout. 21 new tests, all 46 passing, tsc clean.

title: Click task → slide-in detail panel showing changes
description:
When a user clicks a task node, a detail panel slides in from the right side showing all changes for that task.

### Behavior
- Click a task node → panel slides in from the right (~350-400px wide)
- Panel shows: task name, diff state badge, and a list of all changes
- Each change entry shows: field path, old value, new value, action type
- Click outside panel or click an X button → panel closes
- Clicking a different task updates the panel contents
- Clicking a job container node could show job-level changes

### Data
The `changes` field is already computed and attached to each node's data (`DagNodeData.changes`). It's a `Record<string, ChangeDesc>` where ChangeDesc has old/new values and action.

### Files to create/modify
- `src/components/detail-panel.tsx` — new component for the slide-in panel
- `src/App.tsx` — add state for selected node, render panel conditionally
- `src/hooks/use-selected-node.ts` — optional hook to manage selection state
- `src/components/index.ts` — barrel export

### Styling
Dark theme consistent with existing zinc-950 background. Panel should have a subtle border-left separator. Changes displayed in a clean diff-like format.
close_reason: Detail panel implemented and verified in browser

title: Color edges to match diff state of connected nodes
description: New edges (connecting added nodes) should be green, removed edges should be red with reduced opacity, matching the node diff-state styling. Currently all edges render in the default grey regardless of whether the connected resources are new, removed, or modified.

title: Connect trigger_quality task to data_quality_pipeline job
description: The trigger_quality task node should have an edge connecting it to the data_quality_pipeline job, but it currently renders disconnected. Investigate whether the edge is missing from the plan JSON parsing or from the graph building step.

title: Fix dagre/graphlib dynamic require incompatibility with Bun bundler
description: dagre v2 ESM bundle wraps a CJS require('@dagrejs/graphlib') in a compat shim. Bun serve() bundler cannot trace this dynamic require at bundle time, causing a runtime error in the browser. Fix: pre-bundle dagre+graphlib into a single self-contained ESM file using bun build, then import that from layout-graph.ts.
close_reason: Vendored dagre CJS as ESM; build/tests/types all pass

title: Job container node + left-to-right DAG layout
description:
Transform the current flat node layout into a Databricks-style DAG:

1. **Job as group/container node**: Use React Flow's group node (parentId) so the job is a labeled container with task nodes rendered inside it. The job node should show the job name as a header and have a border styled by its diff state.

2. **Left-to-right ELK layout**: Change `elk.direction` from `DOWN` to `RIGHT`. Tune spacing so the primary dependency chain flows horizontally and branching/parallel tasks fan vertically.

3. **Smooth curved edges**: Use React Flow's `smoothstep` or `bezier` edge type instead of default straight edges.

4. **Linear preference**: Configure ELK to prefer linear/straight paths for the longest chain, pushing branches to the side.

### Files to modify
- `src/graph/layout-graph.ts` — flip direction, add group node support, adjust spacing
- `src/graph/build-plan-graph.ts` — set parentId on task nodes pointing to job node
- `src/components/job-node.tsx` — restyle as container (larger, acts as boundary)
- `src/components/task-node.tsx` — update handle positions (Left/Right instead of Top/Bottom)
- `src/App.tsx` — update edge options for smooth curves
- Tests need updating for new layout direction and group node structure

### Reference
Databricks job overview DAG: left-to-right flow, linear spine, vertical branching.
close_reason: Job container + LTR layout complete. Lazy ELK init fixes browser rendering.

title: V1 Vision: Databricks-style plan visualizer
description:
## Vision

Dagshund V1 is a local tool that accepts piped `databricks bundle plan -o json` output, opens a browser window, and shows the plan as an interactive DAG with diff highlighting.

Eventually this becomes a VS Code plugin, but V1 is browser-based.

### Visual Model (inspired by Databricks job DAG UI)

- **Job as container**: Each job is a labeled container/group node. Tasks live inside it.
- **Tasks as DAG**: Tasks are rounded-rectangle cards connected by smooth curved edges showing dependency relationships.
- **Left-to-right layout**: The primary dependency chain flows horizontally left→right. Branching/parallel tasks fan vertically (up/down) off the main spine. Prefer linear edge paths.
- **Reference**: Databricks job overview UI — see `/tmp/databricks-dag.png` or https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/assets/images/example-job-overview-3afc40004602e65c871bd67edfa87f9b.png

### Diff Color Coding

- **Green** (border + subtle bg): New component (added)
- **Yellow/Amber** (border + subtle bg): Changed component (modified)
- **Red + reduced opacity (40%)**: Deleted component (removed)
- **Neutral**: Unchanged component

### Interaction Model

- **Click task** → Right-side slide-in detail panel showing all changes for that task
- **Hover task** → Visual highlighting of the task and its immediate dependency edges
- Pan, zoom, minimap for navigation

### Data Flow

```
databricks bundle plan -o json | dagshund
  → Bun server reads stdin, serves frontend
  → Frontend fetches /api/plan
  → Zod parse + validate
  → Transform to graph model
  → ELK layout (left-to-right, layered)
  → React Flow render with custom nodes
```

### What's Done

- Stdin pipe → server → browser auto-open
- Zod parsing and validation
- Graph model (Plan → nodes + edges)
- Diff state resolution and color coding
- ELK layout (currently top-to-bottom, needs flip)
- React Flow rendering with custom job/task nodes
- Tests (51 passing, 127 assertions)

### What's Remaining for V1

1. Job as container node (React Flow group/parent)
2. Left-to-right layout with linear preference
3. Click task → detail panel
4. Hover task → highlighting
close_reason: V1 feature set complete: DAG visualization, diff highlighting, detail panel, ELK layout, hover interactions all shipped

title: Center-align nodes on vertical layer grid to eliminate edge backtracking
description: Nodes in the same DAG layer should share the same x-coordinate, forming clean vertical columns. All task nodes are already equal width (200px), so alignment mode (left vs center) is irrelevant — the issue is whether ELK is actually snapping same-layer nodes to the same x. Investigate whether the current ELK config already does this correctly and if not, configure elk.layered.nodePlacement to enforce it. The goal: fan-out/fan-in edges flow cleanly right then split/merge vertically — no S-curves or backtracking wraps.

title: Add multi-agent staff engineer code review
description: Set up a code review workflow using multiple high-experience staff engineer subagents. Each agent reviews the codebase from a different angle (architecture, correctness, performance, style, etc.) and provides feedback.

title: Render DAG with diff-state colored nodes in React Flow
description:
Wire the graph model into React Flow rendering with visual diff-state distinction. Minimal styling — just enough to tell states apart.

DEPENDS ON: dagshund-ebk (graph model must exist first)

DATA CONTEXT:
The graph model produces three kinds of nodes:
- Job nodes (nodeKind='job'): represent the resource itself, contain metadata (name, triggers, compute)
- Task nodes (nodeKind='task'): individual tasks within a job, form the DAG via depends_on edges
- The user wants to see BOTH: the job as context/container and the tasks as the interactive DAG

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Custom React Flow node components:
   - src/components/task-node.tsx — renders a task node with diff-state color coding:
     - added: green border/accent
     - removed: red border, reduced opacity (40%)
     - modified: amber/yellow accent
     - unchanged: neutral/default (zinc/gray)
   - src/components/job-node.tsx — renders job metadata (name, etc.) as a header/container node
   Keep it simple — colored border or background tint is enough for now. No fancy visuals yet.

2. Update App.tsx to:
   - Accept a Plan object as prop or via context
   - Call graph model functions to get React Flow nodes/edges
   - Pass custom node types to ReactFlow
   - For now, use a hardcoded test plan or accept via window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ global (stdin feature will wire this up)

3. Custom hook (src/hooks/use-plan-graph.ts):
   - Takes Plan, returns { nodes, edges } ready for React Flow
   - Memoized with useMemo

CONSTRAINTS:
- Function components only, no classes
- All readonly where possible
- Register custom node types properly with React Flow nodeTypes (must be stable reference, not inline object)
- Must handle empty plan gracefully (no nodes = empty canvas, not a crash)
- Dark background (zinc-950 already set in App.tsx) — colors should work on dark
- Handle multiple jobs producing separate sub-graphs within the same canvas
close_reason: Custom job/task nodes with diff-state coloring, usePlanGraph hook, App wired with mixed-plan fixture. 51 tests passing, tsc clean.

title: Hover task → highlight node and dependency edges
description:
When a user hovers over a task node, visually highlight that task and its immediate dependency edges.

### Behavior
- Mouse enters task node → highlight the node (brighter border or glow) and all edges directly connected to it (both incoming and outgoing)
- Non-connected edges and nodes should dim slightly to create contrast
- Mouse leaves → restore normal styling
- Should feel responsive (no animation delay on enter, subtle fade on leave is OK)

### Implementation approach
- Use React Flow's `onNodeMouseEnter` / `onNodeMouseLeave` callbacks
- Track hovered node ID in state
- Apply conditional CSS classes to edges based on whether they connect to the hovered node
- Could use React Flow's `useEdges` or custom edge styling

### Files to modify
- `src/App.tsx` or `src/hooks/` — hover state management
- `src/components/task-node.tsx` — hover visual feedback on the node itself
- Edge styling in App.tsx or a custom edge component

### Styling
Highlighted edges: brighter color (white or accent). Dimmed elements: reduced opacity. Keep it subtle — this is a wayfinding aid, not a light show.
close_reason: Suppression moved to inline script in index.html; error overlay no longer fires
